evals.prompt: 我想给我的项目加个通知系统，用户能收到各种提醒
evals.expected_output: Diagnose as Problem unclear, ask focused clarifying questions one at a time, surface assumptions and blind spots, converge to scoped problem statement before proposing solutions.
expectations: The response diagnoses the request as ambiguous (problem unclear) rather than immediately proposing a solution
The response asks at most one focused clarifying question rather than dumping a checklist
The response surfaces at least one assumption or blind spot (e.g., delivery channel, trigger conditions, who receives notifications)
The response does NOT jump to architecture or implementation choices before clarifying goals

evals.prompt: 我要做一个完整的用户系统，包括注册登录、权限管理、用户画像分析和社交关系
evals.expected_output: Recognize multiple independent subsystems, propose decomposition, ask user to confirm which sub-project to tackle first, avoid brainstorming all subsystems at once.
expectations: The response recognizes the request spans multiple independent subsystems
The response proposes a decomposition listing at least 3 sub-projects (e.g., auth, permissions, profile/analytics, social)
The response asks the user to confirm which sub-project to start with rather than diving into all of them
The response does NOT brainstorm or design all subsystems simultaneously

evals.prompt: 我需要给现有的 REST API 加实时推送能力，数据变更时客户端能立即收到更新
evals.expected_output: Diagnose as Solution unclear, compare 2-3 viable approaches (WebSocket, SSE, polling), lead with recommendation and trade-offs, ask user to confirm approach before proceeding.
expectations: The response diagnoses this as a solution-clarity problem (approach comparison needed) rather than a goal-clarity problem
The response compares at least 2 viable technical options (e.g., WebSocket, SSE, long-polling)
The response leads with a concrete recommendation and explains trade-offs
The response asks the user to confirm the chosen approach before moving to design

evals.prompt: 我要做一个支持十万人同时在线的协同文档
evals.expected_output: Diagnose as Problem unclear despite scale language, clarify workload shape (read-heavy vs write-heavy), budget/constraints, tech preferences before suggesting architecture. Avoid jumping to CRDT/OT/database choices prematurely.
expectations: The response does NOT immediately jump to architecture choices like CRDT, OT, or specific databases
The response asks about workload shape (read vs write patterns, concurrency expectations)
The response asks about constraints such as budget, team preferences, or deployment environment
The response treats the 100k concurrent users figure as a constraint to clarify, not as a spec to immediately design around
